organic_controls: Remove heavily infected leaves where practical; Improve airflow and avoid excess nitrogen; Use sulfur-based fungicides or Bacillus subtilis biofungicides only where locally approved for wheat
chemical_controls: Use locally registered triazole or triazole + strobilurin fungicides such as tebuconazole, propiconazole, metconazole, azoxystrobin mixtures according to label and local extension advice
monitoring_and_timing: Scout from lower canopy upward and act early when pustules begin moving toward the upper leaves or flag leaf
safety_note: Rotate FRAC groups, respect pre-harvest intervals, and confirm the product is labeled for wheat in your region before spraying

organic_controls: Rotate away from wheat residue; Remove heavily damaged tissue where practical; Use Bacillus subtilis or Trichoderma-based biological fungicides only where locally approved
chemical_controls: Use locally registered foliar fungicides commonly used for leaf blotch complexes such as propiconazole, prothioconazole, azoxystrobin or mixed-mode products according to label guidance
monitoring_and_timing: Best results usually come from protecting the upper canopy between flag-leaf emergence and heading rather than spraying too early
safety_note: Do not rely on one chemistry repeatedly; match timing and rate to the local wheat label and advisor guidance

organic_controls: Reduce dense canopy growth; Use sulfur, potassium bicarbonate, neem-based products, or Bacillus subtilis only where locally approved and crop-safe for wheat
chemical_controls: Use locally registered fungicides commonly used against powdery mildew such as tebuconazole, metconazole, myclobutanil, or mixed-mode foliar products according to label guidance
monitoring_and_timing: Treat early at first visible white powdery growth and prioritize protecting young upper leaves
safety_note: Do not apply oils close to sulfur sprays, avoid hot-stress applications, and always follow wheat label restrictions

organic_controls: Use crop rotation and residue management; Remove badly affected leaves where practical; Use Bacillus subtilis or other approved biological fungicides only where locally approved
chemical_controls: Use locally registered fungicides used for foliar blotch diseases such as propiconazole, tebuconazole, azoxystrobin, difenoconazole or approved premixes according to label guidance
monitoring_and_timing: Monitor disease progress during humid periods and protect the upper canopy when lesions move upward
safety_note: Use resistance-management rotation, check harvest intervals, and confirm the product is registered for wheat before application
